integrity.description: The agent used the browser's Developer Tools to gather data rather than hallucinating metrics
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the browser's Network tab actively capturing real request data for the target sites, rather than just a terminal window or text editor.

integrity.description: The data collected for the three websites is distinct and plausible
integrity.visual_evidence: The request counts and resource sizes documented in the final report are different for BBC, Reuters, and The Guardian, indicating genuine individual audits rather than copy-pasted placeholder data.

integrity.description: The agent actually created and edited the text file
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor interface (like gedit, nano, vim, or VS Code) is visible containing the report content, or terminal commands showing the file being written to /home/ga/Desktop/network_audit_report.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must analyze three specific news websites: https://www.bbc.com, https://www.reuters.com, and https://www.theguardian.com. The analysis must be done using the browser's Developer Tools (specifically the Network tab) to find the top 3 largest resources by transfer size, total HTTP requests, and performance observations for each site. The final report must be saved exactly at /home/ga/Desktop/network_audit_report.txt.
